title: PRD: Starter lifecycle trace demo
problem_summary: New teams need a concrete example of what good ProductOS lifecycle traceability looks like.
outcome_summary: The starter workspace demonstrates one lifecycle item through release readiness with clear artifacts, gate outcomes, and explicit deferred later stages.
scope_summary: Seed the starter with discovery artifacts, a bounded story pack, acceptance criteria, release readiness, and lifecycle snapshots that demonstrate the full V6 trace shape.
strategic_context_summary: The starter PRD should prove the ProductOS operating model with one explicit item trace rather than broaden into a generic workspace tutorial.
value_hypothesis: A fully linked starter item reduces adoption friction because teams can inspect a coherent discovery-to-release spine immediately.
target_outcomes: Help new teams understand the ProductOS lifecycle trace model faster.
Reduce ambiguity in the handoff from discovery to release readiness.

prioritization.priority_rationale: The starter PRD is the delivery handoff that proves ProductOS can keep one coherent bounded slice across the lifecycle.
prioritization.reviewer_handoff: Design and engineering should review the seeded scope boundaries before using the starter trace as the default onboarding example.

scope_boundaries: Stay focused on one explicit lifecycle trace through release readiness.
Keep later launch and outcome surfaces visible but intentionally deferred.
out_of_scope: A broad tutorial for every ProductOS workflow or artifact.
Claims that the starter covers production launch or outcome loops in depth.
open_questions: Which additional seeded artifacts would materially improve onboarding without making the starter feel bloated?
handoff_risks: The starter becomes noisy if it carries too many parallel examples instead of one canonical trace.
Teams may misunderstand the release boundary if later lifecycle stages are not kept explicitly deferred.
